title: Garlicky Crunchy Brazilian Garlic Stir-fry
description: This egg-free-friendly dish features a garlicky and crunchy twist on traditional Brazilian cuisine, perfect for a cozy date night.
instructions: Finely chop the garlic cloves and green onions.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and toast until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add chopped garlic and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.
If using sausages, add them to the pan and cook until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat if necessary to prevent burning.
Deglaze the pan with Worcestershire sauce, scraping up any residual garlic and cumin bits from the bottom of the pan. Simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly reduced.
Add chopped green onions, continuing to cook for another minute or two until everything is heated through.
Serve hot over steamed rice or as a side dish.
